page: Amazon Web Services c8gd.8xlarge is a compute-optimized server powered by 32 dedicated physical ARM64 cores utilizing the AWS Neoverse-V2 ARMv9 architecture running at 2.8 GHz. The instance features 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio. It includes 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps, all managed via the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier memory performance, with large-block read-only memory bandwidth reaching 384.64 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 170.56 GB/sec. The server achieves a top-tier Geekbench multi-core score of 21489.0 and excels in database operations, scoring 46924.96 in Passmark database tests. Strong performance is also observed in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, software compilation with Clang, and static web serving throughput at 73.23 GB/sec. However, LLM inference for large 70B models is a notable bottleneck, with prompt processing dropping to a weak 5.91 tokens/sec.

Given its ARM-based architecture and high resource density, the c8gd.8xlarge offers high qualitative cost efficiency for multi-threaded compute workloads that benefit from local NVMe storage. The dedicated core allocation ensures consistent performance without hyperthreading overhead. This server is highly suited for database operations, high-throughput web serving, software compilation, and medium-scale data processing tasks, whereas large-scale LLM inference is better suited for GPU-accelerated instances.
description: Amazon Web Services c8gd.8xlarge is a compute-optimized server featuring 32 dedicated ARM64 cores based on the 2.8 GHz AWS Neoverse-V2 architecture. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of memory, 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and a 15 Gbps baseline network interface. Benchmarks demonstrate top-tier memory bandwidth, with large-block reads reaching 384.64 GB/sec, and exceptional database performance, scoring 46924.96 in Passmark database operations. The instance also delivers strong multi-core performance in software compilation and static web serving. However, it exhibits weak performance in large-scale LLM prompt processing. The c8gd.8xlarge provides high qualitative cost efficiency for compute-intensive workloads requiring fast local storage, making it highly suitable for database hosting, multi-threaded data processing, and web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8gd.8xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized server with 32 dedicated Neoverse-V2 cores, 64 GB RAM, and 1900 GB local NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier database and memory performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8gd.8xlarge is an ARM64 server with 32 dedicated cores, 64 GB RAM, and 1900 GB NVMe SSD, optimized for compute and database workloads.
tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized server featuring dedicated physical cores, high-speed local NVMe storage, and top-tier memory bandwidth for database workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated ARM64 physical cores utilizing the AWS Neoverse-V2 architecture at 2.8 GHz.
1900 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage for low-latency data access.
Top-tier memory bandwidth reaching up to 384.64 GB/sec for large-block reads.
Strong multi-core performance in software compilation and static web serving.
Optimized for database operations, multi-threaded data processing, and web hosting.
